name: Wave Generation
description: A procedural pacing system that builds timed enemy or challenge waves from budgets, spawn groups, difficulty curves, and validation rules.
design_notes: Wave generation is structured pressure. It needs wave_index, encounter_budget, spawn_group_id, difficulty_curve_id, cooldowns, player distance constraints, and failure telemetry so waves escalate without spawning unfair or unplayable combinations.
design_purpose: Create scalable combat or survival encounters without hand-authoring every wave.
Vary enemy composition while preserving a readable pacing curve.
Support arena, horde, tower defense, roguelike, or co-op escalation loops.
player_fantasy: The player feels waves building in intensity and adapts to changing enemy mixes, resources, and terrain pressure.

parameters.name: wave_index
parameters.description: Current wave number used for budget, composition, and reward scaling.

parameters.name: encounter_budget
parameters.description: Budget spent on enemy groups, hazards, or special events in the wave.

parameters.name: spawn_group_id
parameters.description: Composition group selected for the wave.

parameters.name: difficulty_curve_id
parameters.description: Curve that maps wave_index and player state to budget and modifiers.

parameters.name: inter_wave_recovery_ms
parameters.description: Rest or preparation interval before the next wave begins.

edge_cases: Spawn validation rejects all candidates because the arena is full, blocked, or visible to the player.
A previous wave has stuck enemies, summons, or offscreen units that prevent wave completion.
Difficulty scaling outpaces resource drops and creates unwinnable attrition.
Multiplayer join/leave changes player count during wave generation.
common_bugs: Wave completion checks only spawned count and ignores enemies converted, despawned, or stuck outside the arena.
Budget rounding always favors cheap enemies and never selects elites.
Spawn groups are selected client-side and desync in networked co-op.
Inter-wave timers continue during pause or cutscenes and start the next wave early.
balancing_notes: Wave intensity should be tuned through spawn timing, composition, and recovery windows, not only enemy health.
Use telemetry for time-to-clear, damage taken, and resource remaining to tune difficulty_curve_id.
Endless waves need caps or rotation rules so the optimal strategy does not become repetitive farming.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear wave counters, preparation cues, and remaining-enemy indicators when waves gate progress.
Allow reduced intensity or longer inter-wave cooldowns in accessibility or difficulty settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Generate wave plans from wave_index and difficulty_curve_id, validate spawn points with NavMesh/camera rules, and track active enemy IDs until completion or cleanup.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep wave definitions as Resources or JSON and use a director node to commit spawn_group_id plans, cooldowns, and completion conditions from deterministic state.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use GameMode or an encounter subsystem as authority for wave plans; EQS can validate spawn positions and Visual Logger should capture budget, group, and rejection reasons.
implementation_notes.web: Represent waves as scheduled events with stable IDs, seeded composition, and active entity tracking so pause, replay, and reconnect preserve wave state.

example_games.title: Vampire Survivors
example_games.note: Uses time-based enemy waves and escalating pressure.

example_games.title: Gears of War 3 Horde
example_games.note: Uses wave-based enemy combat as a mode structure.
